description_long: This is a bulbous, cream-colored ceramic vase with a single, thick handle attached from the upper neck to the shoulder. The surface is matte and slightly rough, with several irregular, dark blotches or painted marks distributed around the body and neck. The neck is tall and flares outward at the rim, which is uneven and slightly wavy, suggesting handcrafting. The base is rounded and stable, with a subtle transition from the body to the foot. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the form, providing a functional grip. The overall appearance is rustic and utilitarian, with decorative dark patches adding visual interest.
description: A cream-colored, bulbous ceramic vase with a single thick handle and irregular dark blotches, featuring a flared rim and rustic finish.
description_view_0: The handle is fully visible on the left, with three prominent dark blotches on the body and neck.
description_view_1: The handle is hidden, showing the flared rim and two large dark patches on the body.
description_view_2: The handle is visible on the right, with three dark marks on the neck and two on the body, plus a small hole near the base.
description_view_3: The handle is seen from behind, with two dark patches on the neck and one on the body, and the seam of the handle attachment is visible.
